title: Zosterophyllanthos acutilabius
intro: Zosterophyllanthos acutilabius, also known as the Tasmanian hair fern, is a small, delicate flowering plant native to the island of Tasmania. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential applications in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Zosterophyllanthos acutilabius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Tasmanian herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, with the most common preparations including a cough syrup and a topical cream for skin irritations.
